terms.definition: Model too simple to capture trainable structure; training and validation error both remain high. Canonical practical definition in this topic; theoretical link is high bias.
Model fits training noise too closely; training error is low but validation error is much higher. Canonical practical definition here; theoretical link is high variance.
Model captures signal without memorizing noise; training and validation performance are both strong and reasonably close.
Difference between validation and training error; a large gap with low training error signals overfitting.
Plot of model performance vs training set size; shows whether more data reduces overfitting or whether underfitting persists.
Plot of performance vs a complexity hyperparameter; reveals underfitting at low complexity and overfitting at high complexity.
